description: Fix platform codebase issues: SVG sanitizer bug, contradictory tool descriptions, hardcoded constant duplication, and edge case improvements.

userStories.title: Fix SVG sanitizer strippedDepth tracking bug
userStories.description: As a plugin developer using SVG icons, I want the SVG sanitizer to correctly handle allowed elements nested inside disallowed elements so my icons render completely.
acceptanceCriteria: When a disallowed element (e.g., `<script>`) contains allowed child elements (e.g., `<g>`), the depth counter correctly tracks open/close tags within the stripped subtree
After the disallowed element closes, subsequent allowed content is NOT stripped
The test case `<svg><script><g></g></script><path d='M0,0'/></svg>` preserves the `<path>` element
The test case `<svg><script>alert(1)</script><rect width='10' height='10'/></svg>` strips the script but preserves the rect

userStories.title: Fix contradictory browser tool descriptions for screenshot and focus
userStories.description: As an AI agent using browser tools, I want consistent tool descriptions so I know whether to call focus_tab before screenshot_tab.
acceptanceCriteria: The `browser_screenshot_tab` description clearly states that the tab is automatically focused before capture (no need to call focus_tab first)
The `browser_focus_tab` description does NOT say focus is 'required before using browser_screenshot_tab'
The two tool descriptions are consistent with each other about the focus requirement
userStories.notes:
Two files with contradictory descriptions:

1. platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/screenshot-tab.ts line 14: Says 'tab is automatically focused before capture' — this is the correct behavior (the implementation calls chrome.tabs.update with active:true).

2. platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/focus-tab.ts line 13: Says focus is 'required before using browser_screenshot_tab' — this contradicts screenshot-tab's own description.

Fix: Update focus-tab.ts description to remove the claim that focus is required before screenshots. It could say something like 'Useful for bringing a tab to the foreground for visual inspection.' Check if there are other browser tool descriptions that reference focus_tab as a prerequisite.

userStories.title: Extract shared constants: DEFAULT_PORT, config paths, artifact filenames
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want shared constants defined once so changes propagate automatically and values stay consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: `DEFAULT_PORT` (9515) is defined in one place (platform/shared) and imported by all packages that use it (mcp-server, cli, plugin-tools, browser-extension)
`ADAPTER_FILENAME` ('adapter.iife.js') and `TOOLS_FILENAME` ('tools.json') are defined in shared and used by all packages instead of raw string literals
Config directory functions (`getConfigDir`, `getConfigPath`, `getExtensionDir`) are defined in shared and imported by mcp-server, cli, and plugin-tools instead of duplicated
No duplicate definitions of these constants remain across packages
userStories.notes:
Multiple constants are duplicated across packages:

1. `DEFAULT_PORT = 9515` appears in 5 locations: cli/parse-port.ts:8, plugin-tools/build.ts:28, mcp-server/index.ts:171, browser-extension/offscreen/index.ts:23, browser-extension/browser-commands.ts:1876. Move to platform/shared/src/index.ts and export.

2. `'adapter.iife.js'` and `'tools.json'` appear as raw string literals in 40+ places across mcp-server, plugin-tools, cli, and browser-extension. Define `ADAPTER_FILENAME = 'adapter.iife.js'` and `TOOLS_FILENAME = 'tools.json'` in shared.

3. `getConfigDir()`, `getConfigPath()`, `getExtensionDir()` are implemented identically in mcp-server/config.ts:60-64, cli/config.ts:9-13, and plugin-tools/build.ts:35-36. Move to shared.

4. `generateSecret()` is duplicated in mcp-server/config.ts:77-81 and cli/commands/config.ts:369-373. Move to shared.

For browser-extension: it cannot import from platform/shared (it's a standalone build). For those files, keep the constants as-is but add a comment noting the canonical source. Or check if the extension build can resolve workspace imports.

Update all import sites after moving. Run the full test suite to verify nothing breaks.

userStories.title: Add toErrorMessage utility and replace 62 instances of err instanceof Error pattern
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want a shared error message extraction utility so error handling is consistent and DRY.
acceptanceCriteria: A `toErrorMessage(err: unknown): string` function is exported from platform/shared
The function returns `err.message` for Error instances and `String(err)` otherwise
At least the 38 occurrences in browser-commands.ts are replaced with `toErrorMessage(err)`
Other high-occurrence files are also updated where practical
No behavioral changes — the output is identical

userStories.title: Fix edge cases: PORT validation, log level parsing, build lock staleness, retry maxDelay
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want proper input validation and error recovery so the system handles edge cases gracefully.
acceptanceCriteria: `Number(Bun.env.PORT)` in mcp-server/index.ts is validated — NaN from invalid PORT values produces a clear error message instead of passing NaN to Bun.serve()
Log level parsing in logger.ts normalizes case (e.g., `OPENTABS_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG` works, not just lowercase `debug`)
The build lock in plugin-tools/build.ts has stale detection — if the lock directory is older than a reasonable threshold (e.g., 5 minutes), it is automatically cleaned up with a warning
The `retry()` utility in plugin-sdk/timing.ts has a `maxDelay` cap (e.g., 30s) to prevent exponential backoff from reaching unreasonable values
userStories.notes:
Four small edge case fixes:

1. platform/mcp-server/src/index.ts line 171: `const port = Number(Bun.env.PORT) || Number(Bun.env.OPENTABS_PORT) || DEFAULT_PORT`. If PORT='abc', Number('abc') is NaN, and NaN || next works correctly via the fallback chain. But if OPENTABS_PORT is also invalid and DEFAULT_PORT is somehow undefined after refactoring, we get NaN. Add explicit validation: `const rawPort = Number(Bun.env.PORT || Bun.env.OPENTABS_PORT || DEFAULT_PORT); if (isNaN(rawPort)) throw new Error('Invalid PORT');`

2. platform/mcp-server/src/logger.ts around line 54: `parseLevel()` should do `level.toLowerCase()` before the switch statement so `DEBUG`, `Debug`, `debug` all work.

3. platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts lines 52-74: The `mkdirSync` advisory lock persists forever if a build crashes. Add stale detection: check the lock directory's mtime, and if it's older than 5 minutes, log a warning and remove it before proceeding.

4. platform/plugin-sdk/src/timing.ts around line 73: The `retry()` function computes delay as `opts.delay * Math.pow(2, attempt)` with no cap. Add `maxDelay` option (default 30_000ms) and clamp: `Math.min(computedDelay, opts.maxDelay ?? 30_000)`.
